title: phlomoides pygmaea
intro: Phlomoides pygmaea, also known as Pygmy Phlox or Dwarf Phlox, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a low-growing, compact perennial with small pink, purple, or white flowers and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, due to its high content of antioxidants and phenolic compounds. Phlomoides pygmaea is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and supporting immune function.
